TRACKING FEDERAL FUNDS INDIAN GRANTS, GAMING REVENUE BOTH ON THE RISE

Ziva Branstetter Tulsa (Okla.) World January/February 2007
Government & Politics

An investigation reveals that federal grants to Indian tribes have continued to increase despite rising gaming revenues, showing no reduction in aid as tribes become more self-sufficient through gambling operations.

Ziva Branstetter investigated the relationship between federal grants to Indian tribes and their rising gaming revenues. Using the Federal Assistance Award Data System, analysis showed no reduction in federal aid despite increased self-sufficiency through gaming. While national gaming revenues surged, federal grants continued to grow significantly, highlighting the continuous federal support amidst burgeoning tribal incomes from the gaming industry.
